content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer **n**, Chef has asked you to calculate the following sum in the most efficient way:

Here **gcd(a,b)** means  ** greatest common divisor** of two integers **a** and **b**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line containing a positive integer n.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the sum of the greatest common divisor of all pairs of integers from 1 to n.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use the concept of the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two numbers. We can iterate over all pairs of numbers from 1 to n and calculate their GCD using the Euclidean algorithm. Then, we sum up all these GCDs to get the final answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2) — because for each test case, we are iterating over all pairs of numbers from 1 to n.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — because we are not using any extra space that scales with the input size.
